questions.question: What is the size of the 'long' data type in Java?
questions.explanation: The long data type is a 64-bit (8 bytes) signed integer in Java.

questions.question: What is the output? int x = 10; x += 5; System.out.println(x);
questions.explanation: The += operator adds the right operand to the left operand. x = x + 5 = 10 + 5 = 15.

questions.question: Which of the following creates an array with values {1, 2, 3}?
questions.explanation: Arrays can be initialized with values using the syntax: int[] arr = new int[]{1, 2, 3}; or int[] arr = {1, 2, 3};

questions.question: What is the result of: 15 / 4 * 2 in Java?
questions.explanation: Operations are left-to-right. 15/4 = 3 (integer division), then 3*2 = 6.

questions.question: What is the default value of a double variable in Java?
questions.explanation: The default value of a double instance variable is 0.0 (or 0.0d).

questions.question: What is the output? int i = 5; while(i > 0) { System.out.print(i + " "); i -= 2; }
questions.explanation: The loop starts at 5 and decrements by 2 each time: 5, 3, 1, then stops when i becomes -1.

questions.question: What is the output? System.out.println(10 / 3.0);
questions.explanation: Since one operand is a double (3.0), floating-point division is performed, resulting in 3.333...

questions.question: What is the size of the 'float' data type in Java?
questions.explanation: The float data type is a 32-bit (4 bytes) single-precision floating-point number.

questions.question: What is the output? int[] arr = {5, 10, 15}; arr[1] = 20; System.out.println(arr[1]);
questions.explanation: The value at index 1 is changed from 10 to 20, so arr[1] prints 20.

questions.question: What is the result of: !true in Java?
questions.explanation: The logical NOT operator (!) inverts the boolean value. !true equals false.

questions.question: What is the output? for(int i=1; i<=5; i+=2) { System.out.print(i + " "); }
questions.explanation: The loop starts at 1 and increments by 2 each time: 1, 3, 5, then stops when i becomes 7.

questions.question: What is the default value of a long variable in Java?
questions.explanation: The default value of a long instance variable is 0L (or simply 0).

questions.question: What is the output? int x = 7; if(x < 5) System.out.println("A"); else if(x < 10) System.out.println("B"); else System.out.println("C");
questions.explanation: x=7 is not less than 5, but is less than 10, so the second condition is true and "B" is printed.

questions.question: How do you copy an array in Java?
questions.explanation: The clone() method creates a shallow copy of the array. arr2 = arr1 only copies the reference.

questions.question: What is the output? int i = 0; do { i++; } while(i < 0); System.out.println(i);
questions.explanation: The do-while loop executes once before checking the condition. i becomes 1, then the loop exits.

questions.question: What is the size of the 'short' data type in Java?
questions.explanation: The short data type is a 16-bit (2 bytes) signed integer.

questions.question: What is the output? int x = 5; System.out.println(x--);
questions.explanation: The post-decrement operator (x--) returns the current value first, then decrements. So it prints 5, and x becomes 4 after.

questions.question: Which statement skips the current iteration and continues with the next?
questions.explanation: The 'continue' statement skips the rest of the current iteration and moves to the next iteration of the loop.

questions.question: What is the result of: false || true?
questions.explanation: The logical OR (||) operator returns true if at least one operand is true.

questions.question: What is the output? int[] arr = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}; System.out.println(arr.length);
questions.explanation: The length property returns the number of elements in the array, which is 5.

questions.question: What is the output? int x = 8; if(x % 4 == 0) System.out.println("Divisible"); else System.out.println("Not Divisible");
questions.explanation: 8 % 4 equals 0, so the condition is true and "Divisible" is printed.

questions.question: What is the output? int[][] arr = {{1, 2}, {3, 4}}; System.out.println(arr[1][0]);
questions.explanation: arr[1][0] accesses the second row (index 1), first column (index 0), which is 3.

questions.question: What is the output? for(int i=10; i>5; i--) { if(i==7) break; System.out.print(i + " "); }
questions.explanation: The loop prints 10, 9, 8. When i becomes 7, the break statement exits the loop before printing 7.

questions.question: What is the result of: (10 > 5) ? "Yes" : "No" in Java?
questions.explanation: The ternary operator evaluates the condition. Since 10>5 is true, it returns "Yes".

questions.question: What is the output? int i = 3; while(i > 0) { System.out.print(i + " "); i--; }
questions.explanation: The while loop prints 3, 2, 1, then stops when i becomes 0.

questions.question: What is the default value of a float variable in Java?
questions.explanation: The default value of a float instance variable is 0.0f.

questions.question: What is the output? int[] numbers = {2, 4, 6, 8}; for(int n : numbers) { if(n == 6) continue; System.out.print(n + " "); }
questions.explanation: The enhanced for-loop iterates through the array. When n==6, continue skips printing 6.

questions.question: What is the result of: true && true?
questions.explanation: The logical AND (&&) operator returns true only if both operands are true.

questions.question: What is the output? for(int i=0; i<4; i++) { if(i==1 || i==3) continue; System.out.print(i + " "); }
questions.explanation: The loop skips printing when i is 1 or 3, so it prints 0 and 2.

questions.question: What is the output? int a = 3, b = 7; int max = (a > b) ? a : b; System.out.println(max);
questions.explanation: The ternary operator checks if a>b. Since 3>7 is false, it returns b (7). So max=7.
